_comment: STATUS LABELS — track the spec-driven workflow lifecycle.
_comment2: Color progression follows lifecycle: gray (early) → green (testing) → orange/purple (release).
_comment3: See docs/CICD.md § Spec-driven workflow for full lifecycle.

name: status:spec
description: Spec being drafted; not yet approved for implementation

name: status:spec-approved
description: Operator agreed scope; implementation can begin

name: status:in-progress
description: Implementation underway; commits reference this issue

name: status:dev-built
description: Dev image published to GHCR; dev-latest prerelease updated

name: status:ready-for-testing
description: Agent's headless smoke (API + UI) passed; operator's hands-on smoke pending. ISSUE STAYS OPEN — see CLAUDE.md ready-for-testing discipline

name: status:testing-complete
description: Operator finished manual smoke test on phantom-vm

name: status:release-approved
description: Operator approved customer release (chat or label)

_comment: COMPONENT LABELS — track which module/area the issue touches.
_comment3: Mirrors the per-service path-filter contract in docs/CICD.md.

_comment: AREA LABELS — feature-aligned, complement component labels (v0.5.18+).
_comment2: Zero or more per issue. Orthogonal to component:* (build-aligned).
_comment3: An issue answers TWO questions: 'which build workflow fires' (component:*) + 'which feature area' (area:*).
_comment4: Color #14B8A6 (teal) — distinct from component blue + scenario multi-color.
_comment5: See docs/CICD.md § Spec-driven workflow → Area dimension.

name: area:approvals
description: approval queue · approval-flow UX · bypass-approvals semantics

name: area:observability
description: /observability/* (logs · events · traces · metrics · cost · runtime-events · pipeline · connectors)

name: area:models
description: model selection · per-job model override · model config

name: area:providers
description: /providers page · Vertex AI / Gemini provider config

name: area:auth
description: login · session · phantom_auth cookie · SecretStore password hash

_comment: SCENARIO LABELS — classify per docs/CICD.md § Change scenarios.
_comment2: Exactly one per issue. Colors match the SVG diagram.
_comment3: Determines versioning (patch vs major) + customer experience + volume policy.

name: scenario:1
description: Code-only · patch bump · volumes preserved · customer uses UI Update OR re-downloads installer

name: scenario:2
description: Installer change · patch bump · volumes preserved · customer downloads new installer

name: scenario:3
description: Backwards-incompatible · MAJOR bump · volumes wiped (with backup) · customer types UPGRADE

name: scenario:docs-only
description: Docs only — rides along with next code release (no separate tag unless substantial)

name: scenario:trivial
description: Typo / comment / whitespace / formatting only — bypasses spec ceremony
